commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: wraith v2 -- 16 bug fixes + Agents of Chaos hardening

Tier 5 (Paper-Informed):
- CS6: Phase output validation + auto-retry on silent censorship
- CS10: Sanitize command output to block prompt injection from targets
- CS4/CS5: Per-agent turn budgets (80-200 per agent, down from 1000)
- CS1: Proportionality rules in all 9 prompts
- CS8: Identity boundary markers around system prompts

Tier 2 (Process Management):
- Bug 6: Cap concurrent john/hashcat at 2
- Bug 7/14: Process manager with signal handlers for cleanup
- Bug 15: validate_file tool for BloodHound zip verification
- Bug 16: BloodHound timeout cap at 5 minutes

Tier 1 (Observability):
- Bug 1/13: Runner force-writes phase completion to session.md
- Bug 2: Mandatory privesc memory writes after each step
- Bug 4: Two-phase logging (before + after) in kerberoast/lateral/privesc
- Bug 9: Background pot-watcher polls /tmp/*.pot every 10s
- Bug 10: Structured report_input.json generated before report phase

Tier 4 (Reliability):
- Bug 3: Consistent --pot=/tmp/{type}.pot in all john commands
- Bug 8: DCSync output path hardcoded + file validation
- Bug 12: S4U/RBCD structured retry (max 3 attempts, impacket only)

Tier 3 (OPSEC):
- Bug 5/11: LSASS dumps to %TEMP%, exfil via SMB, delete, pypykatz parse

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Persist Pi-hole session to disk and clean up on shutdown

Saves session SID to /data/photos/pihole-session.json so it survives
container restarts without consuming a new API seat. Deletes the session
on SIGINT/SIGTERM to free the seat. Fixes 429 "api_seats_exceeded" errors
from leaked sessions accumulating across restarts.

commit.message:
fix: hard delete 343/345 at top of force_sync + wp_die confirmation + v3.0.0

handle_force_sync now opens with:
- wp_delete_post(343, true) + wp_delete_post(345, true) — bypass trash
- SQL DELETE FROM postmeta WHERE post_id IN (343, 345) — scrubs orphans
- wp_die('DELETION TRIGGERED - RELOAD TO SEE NEW POSTS') — confirms execution

Reload after seeing the die message triggers auto-provisioning via the
config hash change (context.json 2.1.2 → 3.0.0), which creates fresh
posts with new IDs and correct capacity meta from the start.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: remove vestigial Flow, OutputConfig, and ObjectStoreConfig from config

The v2 runner publishes directly to NATS JetStream — the old websocket
output config (Flow.Outputs, DeliveryMode, AckTimeout) and ObjectStore
are unused. NATS URL is resolved via --nats-url flag or NATS_URL env.

- Remove FlowConfig, OutputConfig, ObjectStoreConfig types
- Remove "Graph stream address" wizard prompt
- Simplify config to namespace + sources + optional fields
- Update all tests and example configs
- Add semsource binary to .gitignore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(node): resource contribution controls — CPU, RAM, GPU, storage, bandwidth, scheduling

Phase 1 — Config foundation:
- NodeConfig gains 7 new fields: max_concurrent_jobs, gpu_allocation_pct,
  upload_mbps_limit, download_mbps_limit, active_hours_start, active_hours_end,
  active_days — all persisted to ~/.prsm/node_config.json
- max_concurrent_jobs wired into ComputeProvider (was hardcoded at 3)
- gpu_allocation_pct reflected in available_capacity property
- node.py passes all new fields to providers at startup

Phase 2 — CLI flags + configure command:
- prsm node start now accepts --cpu, --memory, --storage, --jobs flags
- New `prsm node configure` command: --show for inspection, --cpu/--memory/
  --storage/--jobs/--upload-limit/--active-hours/--active-days for updates
  Saves to config file immediately; no wizard re-run needed

Phase 3 — Live runtime API updates:
- GET /node/resources: current allocation + live utilization
- PUT /node/resources: update any field on a running node without restart
- ComputeProvider.update_allocation() and StorageProvider.update_limits()
  apply changes in-place and persist to disk

Phase 4 — Active hours scheduling:
- NodeConfig.is_active_now(): normal ranges, midnight wrap-around,
  day-of-week filtering (weekdays/weekend/specific days)
- ComputeProvider._on_job_offer() and StorageProvider._on_storage_request()
  both gate on is_active_now() before accepting work
- 14 tests in tests/unit/test_active_hours_scheduling.py (all passing)

Phase 5 — Bandwidth limiting:
- prsm/core/bandwidth_limiter.py: TokenBucket (async, asyncio.Lock-safe)
  and BandwidthLimiter wrapping upload + download buckets
- StorageProvider and ContentProvider throttle content-serve paths when
  upload_mbps_limit > 0; zero = unlimited (no-overhead fast path)

Enables multi-device scenarios: laptop at night, workstation on weekends,
NAS storage-only, GPU box after-hours — each with independent resource
envelopes and live reconfiguration.

docs(plans): Add Section 35 — Resource Contribution Controls to arch analysis

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix theme not persisting across page refreshes

The ThemeProvider was saving the theme to localStorage and applying
it to the DOM, but never calling PATCH /api/user/preferences to
persist it to the database. On refresh, the server fetch returned
the default "light" theme, overriding the localStorage value.

Now the ThemeProvider persists user-initiated theme changes to the
server via the PATCH endpoint. Uses an isHydrating ref to avoid
writing the default store value back during initial load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Stripe webhook raw body parsing

Move Stripe webhook handler to index.js, registered as a direct app.post()
route with inline express.raw() BEFORE express.json() is registered.
This guarantees express.json() never processes the Stripe request body,
preserving the raw Buffer needed for signature verification.

commit.message:
fix(vpn): copy config to /etc/wireguard/wg0.conf for wg-quick

wg-quick requires either an interface name (looks up /etc/wireguard/)
or a path matching <iface>.conf. Our pool configs have complex names
that aren't valid interface names.

Fix: copy selected config to /etc/wireguard/wg0.conf, then use
'wg-quick up wg0' / 'wg-quick down wg0' consistently.

commit.message:
fix: normalize absolute paths for ref tracking tier-1/tier-2 matching

Tool events provide absolute paths (/home/user/project/src/auth.ts) but
day entries use relative paths (src/auth.ts). Without normalization, tier-1
and tier-2 matching never fires — only keyword-based tier-3 works.

Now strips project_root prefix before comparing, so both forms resolve to
the same relative path. Added 2 tests for absolute path matching.

commit.message:
Fix stanza 2 translation + apply rules 16-17 throughout

- "не выбирать со временем" → "не выбирать время" (mit der Zeit merged)
- путешествия → странствия (shorter, no confusion with путь/Weg)
- zieht...mit = mitziehen (separable prefix, not preposition)
- Следует instead of Тянется (more accurate for mitziehen)
- на белых лугах instead of по белым лугам (auf+Dat = location)
- Apply rule 16: hat...gemacht, hab'...gedacht compound notation
- Apply rule 17: merge "um" with "deine" (absorbed by genitive)

commit.message:
fix: major GPS tracking accuracy improvements for web and native

Root cause: 50m walk only recorded 13m due to multiple compounding
issues. Fixes applied:

Web location tracking:
- Skip watchPositionAsync entirely on web (Web Locks API issues),
  use polling from the start
- Force fresh GPS positions with maximumAge:0 (browser was returning
  cached/stale identical coordinates)
- Filter out exact duplicate lat/lng positions
- Faster polling interval: 3s → 2s

Distance filtering:
- Lower GPS_MIN_MOVE_M from 2m to 1m (2m was rejecting legitimate
  small movements between poll intervals)
- Tighten web accuracy filter from 100m to 50m (100m readings are
  too imprecise to be useful, created wild jumps)
- Add velocity check: reject readings implying >12 m/s (43 km/h)
  which indicates GPS teleport, not real movement

commit.message:
Add dependency protocol, process server, and file monitor

Implements Phases A-C of the FUSE_PLAN — the foundation for
automatic dependency tracking.

Dependency protocol (tup-server/depfile.rs):
- Binary depfile format: access_type(i32) + len(i32) + len2(i32) + paths
- write_depfile/read_depfile for roundtrip serialization
- FileAccess struct with typed constructors (read/write/unlink/rename/var)
- FileAccessSummary: categorize, deduplicate, detect undeclared deps
- System path filtering (/dev, /proc, /usr, .ccache, .git, etc.)
- 7 tests

Process server (tup-server/process.rs):
- ProcessServer with 3 modes: None, LdPreload, Fuse
- Command execution with env vars and TUP_DEPFILE setup
- LD_PRELOAD environment configuration
- Post-execution depfile parsing
- exec_and_verify(): compare actual vs declared deps
- 7 tests

File monitor (tup-monitor/watcher.rs):
- Cross-platform monitoring via notify crate (inotify/FSEvents/kqueue)
- Recursive directory watching
- Event deduplication
- Filtering (.tup, .git, .hg, hidden files)
- watch_for(duration) for timed collection
- 5 tests

Also adds FUSE_PLAN.md detailing remaining phases (D-H) for
LD_PRELOAD, FUSE, Windows DLL injection, and full integration.

402 total tests, zero clippy warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add conditional game title stripping for compound game names

Words like "fuoco" and "fire" overlap with Pokemon type terms,
so they are only stripped when adjacent to their game title
companion ("rosso fuoco" → strip both, "tipo fuoco" → keep).

Conditional pairs: fuoco+rosso, fire+red, leaf+green.
Uses adjacency check on original token list before filtering.

commit.message:
Fix race condition: hold index lock while pushing metadata

Search threads could see a vector in the HNSW index before its
reverse_map/metadata entries were pushed, causing index-out-of-bounds
panics in concurrent read/write scenarios.

Fix: keep the index write lock held until metadata, reverse_map, and
id_map are all updated. Applied to add(), upsert(), and upsert_many().
